difference.nature: Lexical alignment is necessary but NOT sufficient for topical bearing. Coherence, not truth.
difference.derived: Semantic drift via homonymy: the original query trap terms (surplus value, abstract labour, Midas, Letter 79) pulled Marxist political economy. gate=mixed (lexically borderline); readers all-unsupported.
difference.focused: Trap-free, ruler-faithful query — yet STILL a political-economy CC-BY corpus, because the ruler itself is economically worded (logic of capital, libidinal economy, capitalist unconscious) and the open CC-BY literature for those terms is political economy. gate=aligned (lexically on-ruler) BUT readers still abstain (1 robust-core / 29 unsupported); the lone robust-core is a repair-cafe anthropology paper (capitalist abstinence), not Freud clinic — a spurious convergence.
difference.proves: The gate catches gross query drift (derived=mixed) and confirms lexical alignment (focused=aligned), but only the two undecided readers + the measured empty chair reveal that even a lexically-aligned corpus does not carry the thesis. The Freud-clinic-and-capital thesis (Tomsic book; Portuguese/German scholarship) is outside the CC-BY indexed-article perimeter — the THIRD instance of the meta-finding (after LHC) that book-bound / non-English theses are invisible to CC-BY. The same triple chair (pt/de/book) is empty in BOTH regimes.
